Data Upload Module SequiaViz Team 2025-10-08 1 Introduction Welcome to SequiaViz documentationвЂ¦
How to install and run To install and run SequiaViz, follow these steps: Install the required R packages. Set up the directory structure. Upload your data files.
Data requirements Input file types and formats Data organization and directory structure Required and Optional Columns 1.1 Data Upload Process

Data requirements Input file types and formats SequiaViz integrates results from four types of genomic datasets: Somatic Variant Calling Purpose: Exploration and visualization of somatic mutations in tumor samples Required files: .tsv | .vcf file with annotated variants (.vcf is not implemented yet) Optional files (needed for IGV visualization): Tumor DNA .bam + .bai files Normal DNA .bam + .bai files Tumor mutational burden (TMB) .tsv | .xlsx | .txt file Germline Variant Calling Purpose: Exploration and visualization of germline mutations in normal samples Required files: .tsv | .vcf file with annotated variants (.vcf is not implemented yet) Optional files (needed for IGV visualization): Normal .bam + .bai files Fusion Genes Detection Purpose: Exploration and visualization of gene fusions from RNA-seq data Required files: .tsv | .xlsx files with detected genes fusion (.tsv is not implemented yet) Optional files (needed for IGV visualization and row expansion preview): Tumor RNA .bam + .bai files Chimeric .bam + .bai files Arriba .pdf + .tsv files (for embedded report display and quality metrics) Expression Profile Purpose: Exploration and visualization of gene expression in different tissues Required files: .tsv | .xlsx | .txt file with expression data of sample OR files with tissue-matched reference expression data Optional files: Genes of interest (GOI) .tsv | .xlsx | .txt file Data organization and directory structure Recommended Directory Structure project_root/ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ somatic_data/ в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ mutation_loads.tsv # tumor mutational burden table в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ patient_001/ в”‚ в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ variants.tsv # somatic variants table в”‚ в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ tumor.bam # tumor DNA BAM в”‚ в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ tumor.bam.bai # index for tumor DNA BAM в”‚ в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ normal.bam # normal DNA BAM в”‚ в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ normal.bam.bai # index for normal DNA BAM в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ patient_002/ в”‚ в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ ... (same structure) в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ patient_N/ в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ ... (same structure) в”њв”Ђв”Ђ germline_data/ в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ patient_001/ в”‚ в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ variants.tsv # germline variants table в”‚ в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ normal.bam # normal DNA BAM в”‚ в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ normal.bam.bai # index for normal DNA BAM в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ patient_002/ в”‚ в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ ... (same structure) в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ patient_N/ в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ ... (same structure) в”њв”Ђв”Ђ fusion_data/ в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ patient_001/ в”‚ в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ fusions.tsv # detected gene fusions table в”‚ в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ fusion.bam # tumor RNA BAM в”‚ в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ fusion.bam.bai # index for tumor RNA BAM в”‚ в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ chimeric.bam # chimeric BAM в”‚ в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ chimeric.bam.bai # index for chimeric BAM в”‚ в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ arriba_report.pdf # arriba report в”‚ в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ arriba_results.tsv # arriba result table в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ patient_002/ в”‚ в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ ... (same structure) в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ patient_N/ в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ ... (same structure) Alternative Directory Structures Option 1: BAM + BAI files in separate folders project_root/ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ primary_analysis/ в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ DNA_alignment/ в”‚ в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ patient_001/ в”‚ в”‚ в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ mapped/ в”‚ в”‚ в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ tumor.bam # tumor DNA BAM в”‚ в”‚ в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ tumor.bam.bai # index for tumor DNA BAM в”‚ в”‚ в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ normal.bam # normal DNA BAM в”‚ в”‚ в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ normal.bam.bai # index for normal DNA BAM в”‚ в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ patient_N/ в”‚ в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ ... в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ RNA_alignment/ в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ patient_001/ в”‚ в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ mapped/ в”‚ в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ fusion.bam # tumor RNA BAM в”‚ в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ fusion.bam.bai # index for tumor RNA BAM в”‚ в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ chimeric.bam # chimeric BAM в”‚ в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ chimeric.bam.bai # index for chimeric BAM в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ patient_N/ в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ ... в””в”Ђв”Ђ secondary_analysis/ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ somatic_data/ в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ mutation_loads.tsv # tumor mutational burden table в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ patient_001/ в”‚ в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ variants.tsv # somatic variants table в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ patient_N/ в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ ... в”њв”Ђв”Ђ germline_data/ в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ patient_001/ в”‚ в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ variants.tsv # germline variants table в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ patient_N/ в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ ... в”њв”Ђв”Ђ fusion_data/ в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ patient_001/ в”‚ в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ fusions.tsv # detected gene fusions table в”‚ в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ arriba_report.pdf # arriba report в”‚ в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ arriba_results.tsv # arriba result table в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ patient_N/ в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ ... в””в”Ђв”Ђ expression_data/ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ genes_of_interest.tsv # table of genes of interest в”њв”Ђв”Ђ patient_001/ в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ expression.tsv # gene expression table в””в”Ђв”Ђ patient_N/ в””в”Ђв”Ђ ... Option 2: All files in patient directories project_root/ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ genes_of_interest.tsv # table of genes of interest в”њв”Ђв”Ђ mutation_loads.tsv # tumor mutational burden table в”њв”Ђв”Ђ patient_001/ в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ somatic_variants.tsv # somatic variants table в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ germline_variants.tsv # germline variants table в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ fusions.tsv # detected gene fusions table в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ tumor.bam # tumor DNA BAM в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ tumor.bam.bai # index for tumor DNA BAM в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ normal.bam # normal DNA BAM в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ normal.bam.bai # index for normal DNA BAM в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ fusion.bam # tumor RNA BAM в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ fusion.bam.bai # index for tumor RNA BAM в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ chimeric.bam # chimeric BAM в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ chimeric.bam.bai # index for chimeric BAM в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ arriba_report.pdf # arriba report в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ arriba_results.tsv # arriba result table в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ expression.tsv # gene expression table в”њв”Ђв”Ђ patient_002/ в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ ... в””в”Ђв”Ђ patient_N/ в””в”Ђв”Ђ ... File Naming General Rules Use consistent naming across all patients Avoid special characters and spaces Use underscores or dots as separators All file names must be unique within their directory All per-patient files must have patient ID and dataset type (somatic, germline, fusion, expression|RNAseq) somewhere in their path name Examples: project_root/patient_001/somatic_variants.tsv or project_root/somatic_data/patient_001_variants.tsv project_root/patient_001/expression.tsv or project_root/RNAseq/patient_001.tsv Note: GOI and TMB files are not considered per-patient files because they contain information about all samples Specific Patterns Variants: No specific patterns for this file Fusions: File cannot contain the strings arriba or STAR in their filename. These strings are exclusively reserved for Arriba and STARFusion files Arriba files: For each .pdf arriba report there must be a corresponding .tsv arriba results table Files cannot contain the strings discarded or STAR in their filename. These strings are exclusively reserved for arriba_discarded.tsv and STARfusion files Expression: In case of multiple tissue-matched reference expression data, files must contain {tissue_name} otherwise the name must contain {patient_id} Files cannot contain the strings report or genes_of_interest in their filename. These strings are exclusively reserved for Arriba and GOI files BAM + BAI: For each file.bam there must be corresponding file.bam.bai or file.bai BAM and BAI files must be named the same pattern in the same directory Tumor RNA BAM files cannot contain the strings chimeric or transcriptome in their filename. These strings are exclusively reserved for chimeric BAM files GOI: File must be named as genes_of_interest File must be placed in the root directory of expression data File is common to all samples TMB: File must be named as TMB or mutation_loads File must be placed in the root directory of somatic data File is common to all samples Examples of genes_of_interest and TMB files: project_root/ # root directory for both somatic and expression data в”њв”Ђв”Ђ genes_of_interest.tsv # table of genes of interest в”њв”Ђв”Ђ mutation_loads.tsv # tumor mutational burden table в”њв”Ђв”Ђ patient_001/ в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ somatic_variants.tsv в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ expression.tsv в””в”Ђв”Ђ patient_N/ в””в”Ђв”Ђ ... or project_root/ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ somatic_data/ # root directory for somatic data в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ mutation_loads.tsv # tumor mutational burden table в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ patient_001/ в”‚ в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ variants.tsv в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ patient_N/ в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ ... в””в”Ђв”Ђ expression_data/ # root directory for expression data в”њв”Ђв”Ђ genes_of_interest.tsv # table of genes of interest в”њв”Ђв”Ђ patient_001/ в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ expression.tsv в””в”Ђв”Ђ patient_N/ в””в”Ђв”Ђ ... Required and Optional Columns Somatic variants (.tsv) Required columns: Column Description var_name Variant identifier sample Sample ID in_library Number of times the var_name is observed in the sample cohort (added automatically) TMB (.tsv) Required columns: Column Type Description patient char Patient ID TMB num Tumor mutational burden value Example mutation_loads.tsv patient TMB P001 0.17 P002 0.74 Germline variants (.tsv) Fusion Genes (.xlsx) Data must contain results from Arriba and StarFusion callers. If a column named chrom1 and chrom2 are present, it is automatically renamed to chr1 and chr2. Required columns: Column Type Description gene1 char gene name of first fusion partner gene2 char gene name of second fusion partner chr1 char chromosome of first fusion partner (with вЂњchrвЂќ prefix) chr2 char chromosome of second fusion partner (with вЂњchrвЂќ prefix) pos1 num genomic position of first fusion partner pos2 num genomic position of second fusion partner arriba.called boolean whether Arriba called the fusion (true/false) starfus.called boolean whether STARFusion called the fusion (true/false) overall_support num overall support for the fusion arriba.confidence char confidence score from Arriba (high/medium/low) Expression Data (.tsv) Required columns: Column Description sample Sample ID tissue Tissue name pathway Pathway name (if available) additional Columns with expression values GOI (.tsv) Required columns: Column Type Description gene char Gene name Optional columns: Column Type Description pathway char Pathway name Note: If column pathway is not available, app will automatically use pathways from KEGG DB Example genes_of_interest.tsv gene pathway BRCA1 DNA damage/repair TP53 RTK Signaling 1.1 Data Upload Process This module is the entry point of the entire SequiaViz application. Before uploading data, make sure you have properly formatted input files and directories according to the specifications below. 1.1.1 Step 1: Directory, patient and dataset selection Select main directory containing data for all patients Add patient IDs - you can enter several at once separated by commas or new lines Select analysis types you want to visualize Set patterns for BAM files (e.g., вЂњtumorвЂќ, вЂњnormalвЂќ, вЂњchimericвЂќ) Enter tissue names for expression analysis (e.g., вЂњBloodвЂќ, вЂњLiverвЂќ) 1.1.2 Step 2: Validation and confirmation Application searches the selected directory and finds all relevant files Displays overview of found files for each patient Marks problems using colored icons: рџџў Green: All required files found рџџ  Orange: Optional files missing (e.g., BAM for IGV) рџ”ґ Red: Required files missing or too many files Confirm configuration to continue
